channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: [🗄️DataBase] N+1 Query Problem
item.description: 在做後端開發時，一定遇過這種情況： 👉 API 很單純 👉 查的資料也不多 👉 但效能就是「莫名其妙很慢」 認真查 log 才發現，SQL 被打了幾十次、幾百次，但你明明只寫了一個查詢 這種問題，通常不是你寫錯邏輯，而是踩到了N+1 Query Problem 所以什麼是 N+1 Query Problem 呢? 指的就是 為了取得一組資料，系統額外又發出了 N 次資料庫查詢...

item.title: RPGMapEditor.com: A Free Browser-Based Battle Map Maker for D&D and VTTs
item.description: Making a good tabletop RPG map should not require installing a heavy desktop app, learning a complex tool, or spending an hour just to draw a simple dungeon room. That is the idea behind...

item.title: We've Seen This Before: What SOA Teaches Us About APIs in the Age of Agents
item.description: In the first article in this series, I argued that agents do not replace APIs. They expose the quality of the APIs underneath them. That should feel familiar, because we have been here before. The...

item.title: We Built NeuralDrive — an AI-Powered Emergency Routing System on AWS in One Day — Top 10 at One With AI Hackathon
item.description: Hey Dev community! 👋 Our 4-person team just made it to the Top 10 of the One With AI Hackathon by Arthurite Integrated x AWS. Here's our quick stack: Frontend: React + Google Maps Backend: AWS...

item.title: Building safer AI-agent workflows for the MEXC API
item.description: I’ve been working on a small open-source repo for AI-agent workflows around the MEXC API. The project is not meant to be a “trading bot.” The goal is more specific: to give coding agents like...

item.title: I created two ghosts during lunch. The AI gave one a job offer.
item.description: This is a story about a company that rolled out an AI interview system — and the lunch break I spent creating two ghosts to test it. One of them stuffed its answers with keywords and got a...

item.title: Dynamic OG Images in Rails
item.description: Every blog post, product page, and profile in a Rails app deserves its own Open Graph image, the picture that shows up when someone shares the link on Twitter, LinkedIn, or Slack. The catch is...

item.title: How I revived a 128MB RAM NAS by building my own control panel
item.description: How I revived a 128MB RAM NAS by building my own control panel It all started with boredom and old hardware I had an old Netgear Stora MS2000 sitting around — a box with just 128 MB of RAM. I had...

item.title: Flash Attention: what it does and why it matters
item.description: Flash Attention: what it does and why it matters Your training job is paying for an A100 at $3/hour. The loss is going down, gradients are flowing, and the model's loss curve looks...

item.title: Technical SEO: A Simple Introduction
item.description: You may have heard that there are three types of SEO: On-page SEO, Off-page SEO, and Technical SEO. However, there are only two types: On-page SEO and Off-page SEO, with Technical SEO being a...

item.title: NSO Group Hacking WhatsApp Despite Court Order
item.description: WhatsApp has caught the NSO Group phishing its users, in violation of a court order.

item.title: BYD to install thousands of 5-minute EV chargers across Europe
item.description: Chinese EV colossus BYD has announced plans to speed up its conquest of the European auto market with the rollout of superfast Flash Chargers across the continent. BYD has already installed the...

item.title: Xbox exploring ‘radically different’ console business models
item.description: The RAMageddon crisis has got Microsoft rethinking its Xbox console hardware business. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma and Xbox strategy chief Matthew Ball have both revealed this week that Microsoft is...

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description: Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem. Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report...

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Sneaks Into the World Cup Thanks to Google Gemini
item.description: The Argentine national team will be Google’s test bench and technological showcase during the World Cup.

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release. Of...

item.title: Microsoft is disabling Office 2019 for Mac next month
item.description: Microsoft's Office 2019 apps for Mac will stop working next month, because the company isn't renewing a certificate that validates Office licenses. Owners of Office 2019 for Mac are being warned...

item.title: The “steroid olympics” were a circus—and a window into our culture
item.description: Testosterone. Methenolone. Nandrolone. Human growth hormone and EPO. Meldonium, modafinil, and mixed amphetamine salts. Clomiphene, anastrozole, levothyroxine, and liothyronine. Patches and...

item.title: Waymo built a virtual driver to study how humans react to surprises on the road
item.description: Waymo has a lot of experience building virtual systems to help its autonomous vehicles better understand the real world. It built realistic 3D worlds to better anticipate natural disasters and...

item.title: WhatsApp ordered to host rival AI assistants for free
item.description: Meta has been ordered by the European Commission to restore free WhatsApp access for chatbots made by rival AI providers while the regulator finishes its antitrust investigation. The rare interim...

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards
item.description: On June 9, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, the most capable model it has ever made, generally available. It also did something unusual: it shipped one model as two products, split not by...

item.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance
item.description: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.

item.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
item.description: ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances. "On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied...

item.title: Logitech’s new Mobi Fold squeezes a lot of functionality into a tiny folding mouse
item.description: Logitech finally announced its new ultraportable travel mouse following leaked marketing images that spoiled the surprise last month. As the name implies, the Mobi Fold is a compact mouse that can...

item.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
item.description: The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named...

item.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited,...

item.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
item.description: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.

item.title: From data to decisions: how LSEG is scaling trusted AI
item.description: See how LSEG uses OpenAI to scale trusted AI across its global business, accelerating insights, shrinking release cycles, and empowering 4,000 employees.

item.title: I tried Siri AI, and so far it actually works
item.description: Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer onto their calendar in one shot. And I...

item.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
item.description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.
